title: Review the recent x-auto runtime change for short note-lead exemption in /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto. Focus on approval_guard.py and the newly added/modified tests around note leads. Look for correctness holes, regressions, or overly broad exemption logic. Do not modify files; report concise findings...
summary_text: 1. **High: stale `Reply URLs` is not cleared on normal page updates.** Evidence: [`_upsert_post` patches only generated properties](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/apply_post_package.py:135), and [`_post_record_to_notion_properties` only emits `Reply URLs` when truthy](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:411). If `reply_url` is now empty/missing, Notion keeps old `Reply URLs` content. Minimal fix: in [`_post_record_to_notion_properties`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:384), emit `{"Reply URLs": {"rich_text": []}}` when `reply_url` is absent/empty for update paths (and similarly consider `Source URLs: {"url": None}` for parity). Minimal tests: add a test in [`tests/test_apply_post_package.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_apply_post_package.py) asserting `_upsert_post(..., page_id)` sends `Reply URLs` clear payload when input has no/empty `reply_url`. 2. **Medium: note-lead short-text exemption is still broader than intended.** Evidence: [`_is_note_lead_post`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py:42) allows exemption when there is exactly one note URL, but it does not require that it is the *only* URL; probe confirmed short text with note URL + extra non-note URL still passes (`can_approve == True`). Minimal fix: tighten condition to require exactly one URL total and that URL is `note.com` (or equivalent strict lead pattern). Minimal tests: add to [`tests/test_approval_guard.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_approval_guard.py): short post with `note.com` + `https://example.com/...` after cutoff should be blocked with `text too short`. 3. **Low: created_at cutoff logic is now fail-closed/date-based (good), but tests are not aligned.** Evidence: [`_requires_min_text_gate`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py:62) now parses date and defaults to gate-on for missing/invalid `created_at`. Running `pytest -q tests/test_approval_guard.py` currently shows failures because several “pass” tests omit `created_at` and now hit the 800-char gate. Minimal fix: no logic rollback needed; update tests to match intended policy. Minimal tests: explicitly add/adjust cases for missing and malformed `created_at` (both should enforce min-text gate), and include `created_at` before cutoff where short-text pass is expected. Workflow note: this review was performed in a single lane; multi-lane orchestration was not engaged.
